task: Add a usedFraction(): number method to CostTracker in packages/engine/src/cost-tracker.ts that returns total/limit as a value in [0,1], returning 0 when the limit is Infinity (unlimited) and 0 when the limit is 0 and total is 0
diff:
diff --git a/.bollard/.gitignore b/.bollard/.gitignore
index 56c6e1f..1925d34 100644
--- a/.bollard/.gitignore
+++ b/.bollard/.gitignore
@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
+review-grounding-capture/
 runs/history.db
 runs/.history.lock
 runs/.history.lock.*
diff --git a/packages/blueprints/src/implement-feature.ts b/packages/blueprints/src/implement-feature.ts
index fc671be..bcfa7ab 100644
--- a/packages/blueprints/src/implement-feature.ts
+++ b/packages/blueprints/src/implement-feature.ts
@@ -1717,6 +1717,33 @@ export function createImplementFeatureBlueprint(
           const corpus = buildReviewCorpus(diffText, planForCorpus)
           const result = verifyReviewGrounding(doc, corpus)
 
+          // Temporary capture for review-grounding corpus diagnosis — remove after fixture freeze
+          try {
+            const captureDir = resolve(workDir, ".bollard/review-grounding-capture")
+            await mkdir(captureDir, { recursive: true })
+            await writeFile(
+              resolve(captureDir, `${ctx.runId}.json`),
+              JSON.stringify(
+                {
+                  runId: ctx.runId,
+                  task: ctx.task,
+                  diff: diffText,
+                  plan: planForCorpus,
+                  rawReviewerOutput: raw,
+                  parsedFindings: doc.findings,
+                  kept: result.kept,
+                  dropped: result.dropped,
+                  corpusEntries: corpus.entries,
+                },
+                null,
+                2,
+              ),
+              "utf-8",
+            )
+          } catch {
+            // capture is diagnostic-only — never fail the node
+          }
+
           const proposed = doc.findings.length
           const keptCount = result.kept.length
           const droppedCount = result.dropped.length
diff --git a/packages/engine/src/cost-tracker.ts b/packages/engine/src/cost-tracker.ts
index 169979f..8fd5144 100644
--- a/packages/engine/src/cost-tracker.ts
+++ b/packages/engine/src/cost-tracker.ts
@@ -335,6 +335,18 @@ export class CostTracker {
     return Math.min(Math.max(percentage, 0), 100)
   }
 
+  /** Returns the fraction of the budget used as a value in [0, 1].
+   * Returns 0 when the limit is Infinity (unlimited) or when both limit and total are 0. */
+  usedFraction(): number {
+    if (this._limit === Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY) {
+      return 0
+    }
+    if (this._limit === 0) {
+      return 0
+    }
+    return Math.min(Math.max(this._total / this._limit, 0), 1)
+  }
+
   summary(): string {
     const totalFormatted = this._total.toFixed(2)
     const limitFormatted = this._limit === Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY ? "∞" : this._limit.toFixed(2)

+  tracker.add(50)

+  expect(Number.isFinite(result)).toBe(true)
+})
+
+it('returns correct fraction for various ratios', () => {
+  const testCases = [
+    { limit: 200, total: 50, expected: 0.25 },
+    { limit: 1000, total: 1, expected: 0.001 },
+    { limit: 10, total: 9, expected: 0.9 }
+  ]
+  for (const { limit, total, expected } of testCases) {
+    const tracker = new CostTracker(limit)
+    tracker.add(total)
+    expect(tracker.usedFraction()).toBeCloseTo(expected, 10)
+  }
+})
+
+it('handles very small positive limit', () => {
+  const tracker = new CostTracker(0.001)
+  tracker.add(0.0005)
+  const result = tracker.usedFraction()
+  expect(result).toBeCloseTo(0.5, 10)
+  expect(result).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0)
+  expect(result).toBeLessThanOrEqual(1)
+})
+
+it('returns 0 when limit is Infinity even with large total', () => {
+  const tracker = new CostTracker(Infinity)
+  tracker.add(1000000)
+  expect(tracker.usedFraction()).toBe(0)
 })
 })

plan.summary: Add usedFraction() method to CostTracker that returns total/limit as a value in [0,1], with special handling for Infinity and zero-limit edge cases
acceptance_criteria: usedFraction() returns a number in the range [0, 1]
usedFraction() returns 0 when limit is Infinity (unlimited)
usedFraction() returns 0 when limit is 0 and total is 0
usedFraction() returns total/limit for normal cases (e.g., total=50, limit=100 returns 0.5)
usedFraction() does not modify internal state (idempotent)

risk_assessment.rationale: Low blast radius: new method, no changes to existing methods. High reversibility: simple addition, easy to remove. No financial or security impact. No novel patterns—follows existing CostTracker method style (peek, remaining, etc.).

steps.description: Add usedFraction() method to CostTracker class
steps.tests: Method returns number in [0,1]; returns 0 for Infinity limit; returns 0 for zero limit with zero total; returns total/limit for normal cases; does not modify state
runtimeConstraints: usedFraction() must return 0 when this._limit is Infinity
usedFraction() must return 0 when this._limit is 0 and this._total is 0
usedFraction() must return this._total / this._limit for all other cases
usedFraction() must return a number (not NaN, not Infinity)

steps.description: Create dedicated unit test file for usedFraction() method
steps.tests: Test Infinity limit case; test zero limit with zero total case; test normal fractional cases (0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0); test boundary values; verify idempotency
runtimeConstraints: Test file must follow the pattern of existing cost-tracker-*.test.ts files (e.g., cost-tracker-remaining.test.ts)
Test file must be located in packages/engine/tests/ directory
Tests must verify the method returns values strictly in [0, 1]

non_goals: Do not modify existing test files (cost-tracker.test.ts, cost-tracker-*.test.ts)
Do not modify any other methods in CostTracker
Do not modify the constructor or private fields
Do not change the public API of any other class or module
Do not add usedFraction() to the types.ts PublicCostTracker interface unless explicitly required by a separate task
plan.notes: The task specifies three edge cases: (1) return 0 when limit is Infinity, (2) return 0 when limit is 0 and total is 0, (3) return total/limit otherwise. The method should be a simple read-only accessor similar to existing methods like remaining(), peek(), and total(). Following the project's pattern, a dedicated test file cost-tracker-usedFraction.test.ts is created for mutation coverage.

parsedFindings.finding: Plan specifies creating a dedicated test file 'cost-tracker-usedFraction.test.ts', but the diff modifies 'cost-tracker.adversarial.test.ts' instead, replacing all existing tests for the breakdown() method with tests for usedFraction().

+it('returns correct fraction for various ratios', () => {

+})

 })

Add usedFraction() method to CostTracker that returns total/limit as a value in [0,1], with special handling for Infinity and zero-limit edge cases

usedFraction() returns a number in the range [0, 1]
usedFraction() returns 0 when limit is Infinity (unlimited)
usedFraction() returns 0 when limit is 0 and total is 0
usedFraction() returns total/limit for normal cases (e.g., total=50, limit=100 returns 0.5)
usedFraction() does not modify internal state (idempotent)
